children.name: Chelonia mydas
children.description: The only herbivorous sea turtle; grazes on seagrass; green colour from diet; returns to exact birth beach to nest; critically important for seagrass bed health; IUCN Endangered.

children.name: Caretta caretta
children.description: Massive head crushes molluscs and crustaceans; nests on Mediterranean beaches; occasionally sighted in Swedish waters in warm years; IUCN Vulnerable.

children.name: Eretmochelys imbricata
children.description: Narrow pointed beak extracts sponges from coral reef crevices; spectacular patterned shell historically harvested for tortoiseshell; IUCN Critically Endangered.

children.name: Lepidochelys olivacea
children.description: Most abundant sea turtle; synchronised mass nesting events called arribadas involve tens of thousands of females on a single beach in one night.

children.name: Lepidochelys kempii
children.description: Rarest sea turtle; entire world population nests on one beach in Mexico; recovering after near-extinction in mid-20th century.

children.name: Natator
children.description: Flatback sea turtles; monotypic genus endemic to Australia.

children.name: Natator depressus
children.description: The only sea turtle endemic to Australia, nesting on tropical beaches of the northern coastline and foraging in shallow coastal waters over seagrass and soft-bottomed habitats. Its distinctively flat, olive-grey carapace reduces buoyancy and suits its preference for shallow neritic zones rather than open ocean. Listed as Vulnerable, with all known nesting populations confined to Australian shores.
